mnemonics: SELECT * FROM (Unary) WHERE condition = 'single table'; INSERT INTO Binary VALUES ('two tables', 'like JOINs')
keyPoints: Unary operations (SELECT, PROJECT) work on a single table.
Binary operations (UNION, INTERSECTION, DIFFERENCE, JOIN) require two tables.
Cartesian PRODUCT creates all possible combinations of rows from two tables.
commonMistakes: Confusing PROJECT (selects columns) with SELECT (selects rows).
Forgetting that UNION requires tables to be union-compatible (same number and type of columns).
Using JOIN without specifying a condition, resulting in an unintended Cartesian product.
